Release Pipeline: PyPI + Docker + MCP Registry → GitHub Release

Validation Explain Test Coverage (fixture sets + synthesiser tests + tool layer tests)

nodes.label: redact() CodeQL Neutral Sanitizer Model
nodes.rationale: Declares redact() as a neutral sanitizer to CodeQL so that taint does NOT propagate from argument to return value — eliminating false-positive py/clear-text-logging-sensitive-data alerts on log calls that already pass their payload through redact().

nodes.label: Release: Publish to MCP Registry Job
nodes.norm_label: release: publish to mcp registry job

nodes.label: Overall Status Precedence (critical > warning > invalid > ok)
nodes.rationale: RED module status beats YELLOW in overall_status aggregation; invalid (valid=false, no RED) produces 'invalid' status; critical overrides warning. This precedence ensures the most severe condition is always surfaced.
nodes.norm_label: overall status precedence (critical > warning > invalid > ok)

nodes.label: dryRun=true Enforcement for Validation Explain
nodes.rationale: netlicensing_explain_validation must always send dryRun=true to NetLicensing to avoid side-effects during read-only validation operations.

nodes.label: CodeQL False-Positive Elimination via Neutral Model
nodes.rationale: A custom CodeQL neutral model prevents false-positive py/clear-text-logging-sensitive-data alerts. Without it, CodeQL treats redact(payload) as taint-propagating, flagging already-sanitized log calls.
nodes.norm_label: codeql false-positive elimination via neutral model

nodes.norm_label: aws deployment options (ecs fargate vs app runner)

nodes.label: Per-Request API Key Multi-Tenancy (HTTP mode)
nodes.rationale: A single shared HTTP deployment can serve many tenants by extracting API key per-request via X-NetLicensing-API-Key header, Authorization: Bearer, or ?apikey= query param — without baking a key into the server.
